Standards and certifications Fibres or materials must comply with the third-party best practice standards and require third-party certification or external verification to ensure their compliance. The Fibres Plan includes the standards developed by the Textile Exchange, Canopy, Changing Markets and the Leather Working Group, among others. The full list of accepted standards under our Fibres Plan is included below. Impact analysis The sustainability of the fibre or material is assessed on the basis of Life Cycle Analysis (LCA) methodology, where available. We will continue to publicly campaign on a variety of issues and press for a kinder, fairer society, so that all of us can pay tax into a system we can feel proud of, and which supports everyone in society equally. We have been accredited by the Fair Tax Foundation since 2015 and were the first high street multinational company in the UK to be awarded the Fair Tax Mark. Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 17 Principle: Lush is a Campaigning Company Core Principle: Lush actively campaigns on the issues of human rights, animal protection and environmental justice. All Lush licensed territories are expected to campaign at least once a year on one of the core values through their shops and website. Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 18 PRINCIPLE: LUSH IS A CAMPAIGNING COMPANY Since the beginning, Lush has collaborated with various organizations to run a number of campaigns in-store and online, focusing our efforts on helping people, animals and the planet and fighting for human rights, animal protection and environmental justice. We have been campaigning online and in our shop windows since 2006 and have covered a plethora of issues. In FY2024, we ran a total of 12 campaigns globally. Two were global campaigns, four in the UK & Ireland, four in Europe and two in North America.

We're almost fossil gas-free in retail and gradually electrifying factories. Our North American sites run on 100% renewable energy despite still utilising gas, by supporting recovery of natural gas from a landfill project. In the UK,our main R&D building (Unit 1 Witney) and our EC’s/Perfumes factory operate fully on electricity with Unit 1 also being supplied by 126kWp of solar PV directly from the roof, supplying over 30% of our annual electricity demand. Our Japanese manufacturing facility is already 81% electrified. We are currently performing energy audits in Japan, LED upgrades across UK facilities, remaining non-LED German shops, better monitoring, double doors in the new Glasgow store to improve efficiency, and ‘power down’ campaigns for staff. Next we want to work on more markets moving to deep green tariffs, investments in further reduction of fossil fuel use in Japan, and Taiwan-specific renewable certificates instead of Chinese RECs. Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 64 PRINCIPLE: RENEWABLE ENERGY We're 41% biogas, 59% fossil gas for heating, with our gas-to-electricity ratio at about 30:70. To align to natural cycles, any materials we no longer use should be repurposed, recycled or composted so they can keep generating value. In FY24 we estimate that we generated 7,464 tonnes of total waste globally, improving to 63% diversion rate globally in FY24. This is an increase on the 6,226 tonnes generated in FY22, mainly due to increase in production and sales. Manufacturing globally has a 71% diversion rate. Germany manufacturing had the highest diversion rate in the Lush manufacturing world at 88%, closely followed by North America manufacturing at 82%. Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 76 PRINCIPLE: ENVIRONMENTAL POLICIES Retail sits at 54% on average, with many shops around the world without access to adequate facilities for recycling materials - in North America we have been working with shopping malls to improve access. Wet paper towels are the single biggest source of non-recyclable waste for our shops! We ask our shops to ‘weigh the waste’ once a year so we can collect the data and they can get more familiar with what goes on in their waste. When it comes to product waste, if the product is still usable we make every effort to donate it.
